data_note: Structural opportunity catalog: each entry is a TYPE of opportunity with national on-ramps, tagged by the readiness dimension it fills. A live crawler (scribe companies, hospital volunteer portals, university RA boards, program deadlines) replaces/augments this in a later phase — same pattern as career Dilly's jobs crawler.

opportunities.title: Medical Scribe
opportunities.why_base: Paid clinical hours with real physician exposure — the single densest clinical signal an undergrad can get.
opportunities.how: Apply online (national scribe companies hire year-round); mention your target start date and campus.

opportunities.title: EMT-Basic
opportunities.org_hint: Campus EMS, county EMS, private ambulance
opportunities.why_base: Hands-on patient care with real responsibility — admissions committees read EMT hours as trust under pressure.
opportunities.how: Take an EMT-B course (often at a community college over a summer), then join campus or county EMS.

opportunities.title: CNA / Patient Care Tech
opportunities.why_base: Direct patient care — bathing, feeding, comforting. The least glamorous and most persuasive clinical evidence there is.
opportunities.how: Get CNA certification (community college or Red Cross), apply to hospital PCT roles.

opportunities.title: Medical Assistant (clinic)
opportunities.why_base: Longitudinal outpatient exposure — you see the same patients return, which becomes essay material nobody else has.
opportunities.how: Many clinics train on the job; apply directly to local practices and urgent care chains.

opportunities.title: Hospice Volunteer
opportunities.org_hint: Local hospice organizations (federally required to use volunteers)
opportunities.why_base: Sitting with dying patients is the deepest test of why you actually want medicine — and hospices always need volunteers.
opportunities.how: Search '[your city] hospice volunteer'; onboarding is usually a weekend training.

opportunities.title: Student-Run Free Clinic Volunteer
opportunities.org_hint: Your university's free clinic or nearest med school's
opportunities.why_base: Care for uninsured patients — clinical hours and service to the underserved in one place, with leadership roles to grow into.
opportunities.how: Ask your pre-health office which free clinics take undergrad volunteers; med-school-run clinics often do.

opportunities.title: Shadowing: Primary Care
opportunities.why_base: Schools want proof you know what a doctor's ordinary Tuesday looks like — primary care shadowing answers that directly.
opportunities.how: Ask your own physicians first, then cold-email local practices with 2-3 specific dates you're free.

opportunities.title: Shadowing: a DO physician
opportunities.why_base: DO schools strongly prefer applicants who have shadowed a DO — without it, your DO applications are underpowered.
opportunities.how: Find DOs via the AOA directory or your local hospital's staff page; mention your interest in osteopathic medicine.

opportunities.title: Undergraduate Research Assistant
opportunities.org_hint: Your university's labs (biology, chemistry, psychology, public health)
opportunities.why_base: Sustained time in one lab beats scattered semesters in three — depth produces the poster or authorship that research-heavy schools screen for.
opportunities.how: Email 3 PIs whose recent papers you can say one real sentence about; ask for 15 minutes.

opportunities.title: Summer Research Program (REU / SURP)
opportunities.org_hint: NSF REU sites, university SURPs, NIH SIP
opportunities.why_base: A funded summer of full-time research with a poster at the end — the fastest legitimate research credential that exists.
opportunities.how: Applications open Nov-Feb for the following summer. Apply to 8-12; acceptance rates are low individually.

opportunities.title: SHPEP (Summer Health Professions Education Program)
opportunities.why_base: A funded early-pipeline program for students from underrepresented backgrounds — signals commitment and gives you mentors who write letters.
opportunities.how: Apply by the winter deadline of your freshman or sophomore year at shpep.org.

opportunities.title: Crisis Text/Phone Line Volunteer
opportunities.why_base: High-stakes listening under pressure — interviewers ask about hard conversations, and you will have real ones to tell.
opportunities.how: Apply online; training is 20-30 hours and remote shifts fit around classes.

opportunities.title: Tutoring / Mentoring Underserved Students
opportunities.org_hint: Local schools, Boys & Girls Club, campus programs
opportunities.why_base: Longitudinal non-clinical service — schools screen for sustained commitment to a community, not one-off events.
opportunities.how: Pick ONE organization and stay two years; depth is the entire point.

opportunities.title: Clinical Research Assistant (hospital)
opportunities.org_hint: Academic medical center clinical trials teams
opportunities.why_base: Research and patient contact at once — consenting real patients into studies counts toward both dimensions.
opportunities.how: Search hospital job boards for 'clinical research assistant' or 'CRC'; gap-year versions are full-time.

opportunities.title: Club Leadership (any organization you already love)
opportunities.org_hint: Your existing club — pre-med or not
opportunities.why_base: Leading anything real — a robotics team, a dance org, a food pantry — reads better than a resume-line title in a club you never attended.
opportunities.how: Run for a role with actual responsibility (treasurer, events, president) in something you already show up for.
